domain:
In an academic institution, students enroll in various courses offered by the university. Each student is uniquely identified by a student code. An enrollment in the course requires the specific date on which it occurred for administrative purposes.

Courses are supported by Teaching Assistants (TAs), who are identified by their first and last names. A TA may assist in multiple different courses, and the specific role of a TA within a course is defined by an assignment, which describes the details and responsibilities assigned to them. Regarding courses logistics, they consist of one or more lab sections. Each section occurs at a specific location and time slot with start and end times.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Course and Assignment be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Assignment would depend on Course for its existence.
2. Use association, Assignment could exist independently of Course, and can also be linked to multiple courses.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Assignment depends on Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Assignment exists independently of Course.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Assignment depends on Course.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Course in the relationship with Assignment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an assignment can exist without any associated course.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an assignment must have at least one associated course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one Course.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one Course.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Course and Enrollment be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Enrollment would depend on Course for its existence.
2. Use association, Enrollment could exist independently of Course, and can also be linked to multiple courses.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Enrollment depends on Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Enrollment exists independently of Course.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Enrollment depends on Course.

For example:
1. Use composition, LabSection would depend on Course for its existence.
2. Use association, LabSection could exist independently of Course, and can also be linked to multiple courses.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class LabSection depends on Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class LabSection exists independently of Course.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class LabSection depends on Course.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Course in the relationship with LabSection?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a lab section can exist without any associated course.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a lab section must have at least one associated course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, LabSection can exist without Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, LabSection must have at least one Course.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, LabSection must have at least one Course.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of TeachingAssistant in the relationship with Assignment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an assignment can exist without any associated teaching assistant.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an assignment must have at least one associated teaching assistant.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without TeachingAssistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one TeachingAssistant.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one TeachingAssistant.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of TeachingAssistant in the relationship with Assignment?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, an assignment is associated with exactly one teaching assistant.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, an assignment can be associated with multiple teaching assistants.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each assignment links to one teaching assistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each assignment can link to multiple teaching assistants.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each assignment can link to multiple teaching assistants.

questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,TeachingAssistant must have at least one LabSection.

questions.question:
Should Enrollment be modeled as an association class linking Student and Course, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Enrollment exists only as part of the relationship between Student and Course. A enrollment cannot exist without the link between Student and Course.
2. Regular class: Enrollment is a class that has links to Student and Course. In this case multiple enrollments can exist for every Student and Course.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Student and Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Enrollment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Student and Course.

questions.question:
Should Assignment be modeled as an association class linking Course and TeachingAssistant, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Assignment exists only as part of the relationship between Course and TeachingAssistant. A assignment cannot exist without the link between Course and TeachingAssistant.
2. Regular class: Assignment is a class that has links to Course and TeachingAssistant. In this case multiple assignments can exist for every Course and TeachingAssistant.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Assignment as association class linking Course and TeachingAssistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Assignment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Assignment as association class linking Course and TeachingAssistant.
